basic_details.job_title: TGPSC Town Planning Assistant Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 07 Posts
basic_details.department: Town and Country Planning Department, Telangana
basic_details.short_information: The Telangana Public Service Commission (TGPSC) has released Notification No. 06/G/TP/2026, dated 10/07/2026, for the General Recruitment of Town Planning Assistant in the Town and Country Planning Department. A total of 7 vacancies have been notified across State Level and Multi-Zonal cadres. Online applications open on July 15, 2026, and the examination is tentatively scheduled for October 2026.

important_dates.admit_card_date: 7 days prior to the examination, up to 5:00 PM on the previous day

application_fee.details: The application involves two separate online payments: an application processing fee and an examination fee. | All unemployed applicants are exempted from the examination fee; however, employees of any Government (Central/State/PSU/Corporations/Other Government Sector) must pay it. Payment can be made online via Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI. Fees once paid are not refunded or adjusted under any circumstances.

age_limit.details: Age is reckoned as on 01/07/2026, as per Rule 12(1)(a)(v) of the Telangana State and Subordinate Service Rules, 1996. | Criteria Details Minimum Age 18 years (must not be born after 01/07/2008) Maximum Age 44 years (must not be born before 02/07/1982) | No person is eligible if they are less than 18 years old or have crossed 61 years of age (superannuation age).

post_wise_qualification.essential_qualification: Must possess a B. Planning / B.Tech (Planning)

qualification.details: To be eligible for this recruitment, applicants must satisfy citizenship, character, and educational qualification requirements as prescribed in the notification. | The applicant's character and antecedents must qualify them for the service. The applicant must possess the prescribed academic and other qualifications for the post. The applicant must be a citizen of India. | The applicant must possess the prescribed academic qualification as on the date of notification, i.e., 10/07/2026. | Degrees obtained through Open Universities or Distance Education mode must be recognized by the UGC, AICTE, or Distance Education Bureau, as applicable. The result of the qualifying examination must have been declared by the concerned university as on the date of notification; mere conduct of the exam or processing of results does not fulfil the qualification requirement. | Age is reckoned as on 01/07/2026, as per Rule 12(1)(a)(v) of the Telangana State and Subordinate Service Rules, 1996. | No person is eligible if they are less than 18 years old or have crossed 61 years of age (superannuation age). | Even after availing eligible relaxations, no candidate can exceed the maximum age limit for this post as on 01/07/2026.

salary_details.details: Selected candidates will draw a salary as per the pay scale notified for the post.

selection_process: Selection is based purely on marks secured in the objective-type written examination
Written Examination (Objective Type) conducted via CBRT or OMR-based mode. Qualifying marks: OC
Sportspersons & EWS — not less than 40%; BC — not less than 35%; SC

ST & PH — not less than 30%. Candidates are selected and allotted posts as per merit and order of preference (web-options) against available vacancies. Appointment is subject to being declared medically fit and willingness to serve anywhere in Telangana State.
Merely securing the minimum qualifying marks does not guarantee selection. There is no waiting list for this recruitment.

pattern_table.Paper: Paper-I: General Studies and General Abilities

exam_pattern.details: The written examination is objective type, conducted either in OMR-based offline mode or as a Computer Based Recruitment Test (CBRT), and consists of two papers. | For Computer Based Tests conducted in multiple sessions, the Commission normalizes scores across sessions using a normalization formula, and results are declared on a percentile basis. | Paper-I covers General Studies and General Abilities, including current affairs, Indian and Telangana history, geography, polity, governance, and reasoning. Paper-II covers Planning at degree level, including demography and urbanization, environmental planning, disaster management, urban governance, traffic and transportation planning, planning legislation, regional planning, housing, GIS, and current planning trends. Candidates should refer to the official notification for the complete, detailed syllabus topics.

syllabus.details: Paper-I covers General Studies and General Abilities, including current affairs, Indian and Telangana history, geography, polity, governance, and reasoning. Paper-II covers Planning at degree level, including demography and urbanization, environmental planning, disaster management, urban governance, traffic and transportation planning, planning legislation, regional planning, housing, GIS, and current planning trends. Candidates should refer to the official notification for the complete, detailed syllabus topics.

how_to_apply: New applicants must complete One Time Registration (OTR) to obtain a TGPSC ID; already-registered applicants can log in directly with their existing TGPSC ID.
Click on the Online Application Link, log in with TGPSC ID, Date of Birth, and OTP sent to the registered mobile number.
Verify auto-fetched OTR details such as Name, Date of Birth, Educational Qualifications, Community, Gender, Disability, and Ex-Servicemen/Sports status.
Preview the application and click SAVE & PAYMENT to proceed to fee payment.
Pay the prescribed application processing fee and examination fee (if applicable) via Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI.
Download and save the system-generated PDF application form, noting the Reference ID Number for future correspondence.

Important: OTR is not considered an application by itself; a separate online application must be submitted for this specific notification. No changes to the application are possible after clicking CONFIRM & SUBMIT.
important_instructions: Handwritten, typed, photostat, or outside-printed application forms will not be accepted; only the prescribed online mode is valid. Government/PSU/autonomous body employees must submit a No Objection Certificate from their employer when required. Any electronic gadgets, including mobile phones, smartwatches, calculators, and Bluetooth devices, are strictly banned inside the examination hall. Furnishing false or incorrect information at any stage will lead to rejection of candidature or debarment from future TGPSC examinations. Candidates can raise objections on the provisional answer key online by paying ₹500 per question; the fee is refunded if the objection is upheld. After results, candidates can obtain their Memorandum of Marks online for ₹200 within 30 days; requests for revaluation are not entertained. For technical issues related to application or hall ticket, contact 040-23120301/302 or email [email protected] (10:30 AM to 5:00 PM on working days).

content_sections.heading: TGPSC Town Planning Assistant Notification PDF
content_sections.text: Candidates are advised to read the full official notification before applying, as it contains complete eligibility rules, reservation details, and annexures. Download Official Notification (No. 06/G/TP/2026) from tgpsc.gov.in

content_sections.heading: TGPSC Town Planning Assistant Recruitment 2026: Quick Overview

rows.details: Town and Country Planning Department, Telangana
Objective Type (OMR-based Offline or Computer Based Recruitment Test — to be notified)

content_sections.heading: TGPSC Town Planning Assistant Important Dates
content_sections.text: Candidates should complete registration well before the closing date, as the Commission advises against waiting until the last day.

rows.event: Last Date and Time for Online Application

rows.date: 7 days prior to the examination, up to 5:00 PM on the previous day

content_sections.heading: TGPSC Town Planning Assistant Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: The 7 vacancies are split between a State Level post and a Multi-Zonal post, each governed by different local reservation rules. Note: As per G.O.Ms.No.86, G.A.(Ser.A) Department, Dt. 18/05/2026, the upper age limit for this post has been raised by 10 years, from 34 to 44 years. The number of vacancies is provisional and subject to variation; additions before the exam will be notified through an addendum, and deletions can be made up to the declaration of final selection. "L" denotes vacancies filled through Local Reservation. Local Reservation is not applicable to Post Code 01 (State Level, statewide merit); 95% Local Reservation applies to Post Code 02 (Multi-Zonal), as per Para-8 of G.O.Ms.No.124, G.A. (SPF-MC) Department, dated 30/08/2018.

content_sections.heading: TGPSC Town Planning Assistant Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: To be eligible for this recruitment, applicants must satisfy citizenship, character, and educational qualification requirements as prescribed in the notification. The applicant's character and antecedents must qualify them for the service. The applicant must possess the prescribed academic and other qualifications for the post. The applicant must be a citizen of India. The applicant must possess the prescribed academic qualification as on the date of notification, i.e., 10/07/2026. Degrees obtained through Open Universities or Distance Education mode must be recognized by the UGC, AICTE, or Distance Education Bureau, as applicable. The result of the qualifying examination must have been declared by the concerned university as on the date of notification; mere conduct of the exam or processing of results does not fulfil the qualification requirement. Age is reckoned as on 01/07/2026, as per Rule 12(1)(a)(v) of the Telangana State and Subordinate Service Rules, 1996. No person is eligible if they are less than 18 years old or have crossed 61 years of age (superannuation age). Even after availing eligible relaxations, no candidate can exceed the maximum age limit for this post as on 01/07/2026.

rows.educational_qualification: Must possess a B. Planning / B.Tech (Planning)

rows.age_relaxation: Up to 5 years, based on length of regular service
3 years plus length of service in the Armed Forces
3 years plus length of service in the NCC

content_sections.heading: TGPSC Town Planning Assistant Salary and Pay Scale
content_sections.text: Selected candidates will draw a salary as per the pay scale notified for the post.

content_sections.heading: TGPSC Town Planning Assistant Exam Pattern
content_sections.text: The written examination is objective type, conducted either in OMR-based offline mode or as a Computer Based Recruitment Test (CBRT), and consists of two papers. For Computer Based Tests conducted in multiple sessions, the Commission normalizes scores across sessions using a normalization formula, and results are declared on a percentile basis. Paper-I covers General Studies and General Abilities, including current affairs, Indian and Telangana history, geography, polity, governance, and reasoning. Paper-II covers Planning at degree level, including demography and urbanization, environmental planning, disaster management, urban governance, traffic and transportation planning, planning legislation, regional planning, housing, GIS, and current planning trends. Candidates should refer to the official notification for the complete, detailed syllabus topics.

rows.paper: Paper-I: General Studies and General Abilities

content_sections.heading: TGPSC Town Planning Assistant Application Fee
content_sections.text: The application involves two separate online payments: an application processing fee and an examination fee. All unemployed applicants are exempted from the examination fee; however, employees of any Government (Central/State/PSU/Corporations/Other Government Sector) must pay it. Payment can be made online via Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI. Fees once paid are not refunded or adjusted under any circumstances.

content_sections.heading: TGPSC Town Planning Assistant Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection is based purely on marks secured in the objective-type written examination, followed by merit-based allotment. Written Examination (Objective Type) conducted via CBRT or OMR-based mode. Qualifying marks: OC, Sportspersons & EWS — not less than 40%; BC — not less than 35%; SC, ST & PH — not less than 30%. Candidates are selected and allotted posts as per merit and order of preference (web-options) against available vacancies. Appointment is subject to being declared medically fit and willingness to serve anywhere in Telangana State. Merely securing the minimum qualifying marks does not guarantee selection. There is no waiting list for this recruitment.

content_sections.heading: TGPSC Town Planning Assistant Exam Centers
content_sections.text: Hyderabad (including HMDA Jurisdiction) Karimnagar Khammam Warangal Nizamabad The Commission reserves the right to allot a different centre or create a new one for administrative reasons; requests for centre change will not be entertained.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for TGPSC Town Planning Assistant Recruitment
content_sections.text: PDF Application Form and Hall Ticket Photo ID: Aadhaar Card / Voter ID / Passport / Driving License / Service ID with photograph / Bank or Post Office Passbook with photograph / PAN Card Proof of Educational Qualifications SSC/CBSE/ICSE certificate (for Date of Birth) School Study Certificate (1st to 7th Class) or Residence Certificate, where applicable Declaration by the Unemployed (for exam fee exemption) No Objection Certificate from employer, if applicable Community Certificate for BC/SC/ST, Non-Creamy Layer Certificate (for BCs), or Income Certificate (for EWS), as applicable Disability Certificate in SADAREM format, for differently abled applicants Service Certificate or Ex-Servicemen/Sports certificate, where age relaxation or reservation is claimed

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for TGPSC Town Planning Assistant Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Visit the official website: tgpsc.gov.in New applicants must complete One Time Registration (OTR) to obtain a TGPSC ID; already-registered applicants can log in directly with their existing TGPSC ID. Click on the Online Application Link, log in with TGPSC ID, Date of Birth, and OTP sent to the registered mobile number. Verify auto-fetched OTR details such as Name, Date of Birth, Educational Qualifications, Community, Gender, Disability, and Ex-Servicemen/Sports status. Select the preferred examination centre. Preview the application and click SAVE & PAYMENT to proceed to fee payment. Pay the prescribed application processing fee and examination fee (if applicable) via Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI. Download and save the system-generated PDF application form, noting the Reference ID Number for future correspondence. Important: OTR is not considered an application by itself; a separate online application must be submitted for this specific notification. No changes to the application are possible after clicking CONFIRM & SUBMIT.

content_sections.heading: TGPSC Town Planning Assistant: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Handwritten, typed, photostat, or outside-printed application forms will not be accepted; only the prescribed online mode is valid. Government/PSU/autonomous body employees must submit a No Objection Certificate from their employer when required. Any electronic gadgets, including mobile phones, smartwatches, calculators, and Bluetooth devices, are strictly banned inside the examination hall. Furnishing false or incorrect information at any stage will lead to rejection of candidature or debarment from future TGPSC examinations. Candidates can raise objections on the provisional answer key online by paying ₹500 per question; the fee is refunded if the objection is upheld. After results, candidates can obtain their Memorandum of Marks online for ₹200 within 30 days; requests for revaluation are not entertained. For technical issues related to application or hall ticket, contact 040-23120301/302 or email [email protected] (10:30 AM to 5:00 PM on working days).

faq.question: Q1. When does the online application for TGPSC Town Planning Assistant Recruitment 2026 start?
faq.answer: Online applications start on July 15, 2026.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for TGPSC Town Planning Assistant Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application is August 22, 2026, up to 5:00 PM.

faq.question: Q3. How many vacancies are there in TGPSC Town Planning Assistant Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 7 vacancies have been notified: 2 under State Level (Post Code 01) and 5 under Multi-Zonal (Post Code 02).

faq.question: Q4. What is the educational qualification required for TGPSC Town Planning Assistant?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess a B. Planning or B.Tech (Planning) degree.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for TGPSC Town Planning Assistant Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 18 years and the maximum age is 44 years, as on 01/07/2026. The upper age limit was raised from 34 to 44 years as per G.O.Ms.No.86, G.A.(Ser.A) Department, dated 18/05/2026

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for TGPSC Town Planning Assistant Recruitment?
faq.answer: The application processing fee is ₹1,000 for OC and BC candidates, and ₹500 for SC, ST, and PwD candidates. An additional examination fee of ₹120 applies, except for unemployed applicants who are exempted.

faq.question: Q7. What is the salary for TGPSC Town Planning Assistant?
faq.answer: The pay scale for this post is ₹51,320 – ₹1,27,310/-.

faq.question: Q8. When will the TGPSC Town Planning Assistant exam be held?
faq.answer: The examination is tentatively scheduled for October 2026; the exact schedule will be notified separately

faq.question: Q9. What is the exam pattern for TGPSC Town Planning Assistant Recruitment?
faq.answer: The exam has two objective-type papers: Paper-I (General Studies and General Abilities, 150 marks) and Paper-II (Planning, Degree Level, 300 marks), for a total of 450 marks.

faq.question: Q10. How can I apply for TGPSC Town Planning Assistant Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must apply online through tgpsc.gov.in using their TGPSC OTR ID; no other mode of application is accepted.
